intro: Ischyrolepis marlothii, also known as the dwarf rush, is a low-growing, rhizomatous perennial plant belonging to the Restionaceae family. Traditionally, I. marlothii has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin problems, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of I. marlothii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, I. marlothii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
